title: Make all Atelier tool surfaces parallel-agent safe and Argus steerable
operator_request: Everything should be usable by parallel agents inside Handshake, with front-end and back-end navigation paths and Argus visual proof; missing GUI/operator surfaces are accepted scope expansion.

state_recovery.recovery_rule: Audit every Atelier surface for stable IDs, routes, leases, receipts, and visual proof. Treat gaps as product debt, not documentation-only issues.

scope.summary: Harden CKC, Posekit, Ingest, Facial-backed tools, and prompt feedback surfaces for safe parallel model operation.
base_scope: Audit stable author_id coverage for all Atelier tabs, controls, lists, viewers, and status regions.
Add deterministic backend/headless commands where GUI-only operation blocks agents.
Add receipts/attribution for model actions that mutate CKC/Posekit/Ingest state.
Ensure parallel agents have leases/conflict behavior for shared mutable targets.
Expose enough structured state for no-context recovery.
Update internal UserManual with model operation rules.
acceptance_criteria: Every major Atelier surface has stable Argus-visible author_ids.
Mutations are attributable and safe under parallel agent use.
No critical workflow requires hidden chat context or manual foreground interaction.
Argus can steer and re-observe all core controls.
verification: Parallel-agent mutation tests for selected workflows.
Manual-content tests for model operation guidance.
